interfaces.name: wizard.run() — new harness selection step
interfaces.signature: wizard.py around line ~210 (immediately after frameworks multi-select & answers['frameworks'] is computed; before playwright browser sub-flow)
interfaces.behavior_change: Single-select menu titled 'Select harness:' with three entries: 'Claude Code' (claude-code), 'Codex' (codex), 'opencode  [adds opencode to frameworks]' (opencode). Default index = 0 (claude-code) for new configs. Writes answers['harness']. When chosen value == 'opencode' AND 'opencode' not already in answers['frameworks'], append it and emit click.echo('opencode added to frameworks (required by harness selection).').

interfaces.name: wizard.run_edit() — new harness selection step
interfaces.behavior_change: Same menu shape as run(), but default index = HARNESS_NAMES.index(config.harness) (so the persisted harness is pre-selected). Same opencode auto-add behaviour.

interfaces.name: detect/wizard_integration.run_with_detection() — new harness step
interfaces.signature: wizard_integration.py around line ~190 (after frameworks split, before playwright browser sub-flow)
interfaces.behavior_change: Same menu and auto-add behaviour as wizard.run(). Default index 0 (claude-code) for new configs. Writes answers['harness'].

interfaces.name: detect/wizard_integration.run_edit_with_detection() — new harness step
interfaces.signature: wizard_integration.py around line ~755 (mirrors run_edit_with_detection's frameworks placement)
interfaces.behavior_change: Same as run_with_detection but default index = HARNESS_NAMES.index(config.harness). Same opencode auto-add.

interfaces.name: detect/wizard_integration.apply_detection_to_config() — preserve harness
interfaces.behavior_change: No interactive prompt; the function builds an updated Config by merging detection with existing config. Preserve config.harness through the merge unchanged. (Implementation: this function already passes through fields it doesn't actively change; verify it does not silently drop harness when calling Config(...).)

interfaces.name: merge_detection_with_config() — pass-through harness
interfaces.behavior_change: Verify the merged dict carries config.harness unchanged so that any downstream code that reconstructs Config from the merged dict does not lose the value. (May be a no-op edit if the function already only passes specific keys.)

data_flow: User runs `clauded` (or `clauded --edit`) -> wizard frameworks multi-select -> NEW harness select step (default = claude-code for new, current for edit) -> if user picks 'opencode' and frameworks lacks it, the wizard auto-appends 'opencode' to answers['frameworks'] with an info message -> answers['harness'] = chosen value -> Config.from_wizard(answers) (Story 02 already accepts this key) -> Config.save() emits top-level 'harness:' line. Detect-flow variants (run_with_detection / run_edit_with_detection) inherit identical behaviour. apply_detection_to_config does NOT prompt — it just preserves config.harness through the merge.
files_to_modify: src/clauded/wizard.py — add harness step in run() and run_edit() with auto-add side effect
src/clauded/detect/wizard_integration.py — add harness step in run_with_detection() and run_edit_with_detection(); ensure apply_detection_to_config preserves harness
tests/test_wizard.py — coverage for AC-009 (offered with 3 entries), AC-010 (auto-add opencode), AC-011 (run_edit preselects current harness)
tests/test_detect_integration.py — parallel coverage for the two integration flows + apply_detection_to_config preserve test
CHANGELOG.md — [Unreleased] Added entry for the harness wizard step
implementation_strategy:
TDD per-component:

1. RED: write tests for AC-009/010/011 against wizard.run() and wizard.run_edit() (recording-select-side-effect for the harness menu shape and default index; auto-add assertion).
2. GREEN: edit wizard.py — add the harness step in run() (default 0) and run_edit() (default = HARNESS_NAMES.index(config.harness)); auto-add opencode to frameworks when chosen.
3. RED: write tests for the same behaviour in run_with_detection() and run_edit_with_detection() (paralleling tests in test_detect_integration.py).
4. GREEN: edit wizard_integration.py — add the harness step in both flows.
5. RED: write a test asserting apply_detection_to_config preserves config.harness through the merge (the non-interactive flow).
6. GREEN: confirm or fix preservation in apply_detection_to_config.
7. Add CHANGELOG entry.
8. Run `make check`; coverage >= 80%; all tests green.

Auto-add details:
- The auto-add fires only when the chosen harness is 'opencode' AND 'opencode' is not already in answers['frameworks']. No double info message.
- The info message is emitted once per wizard run (not per call to the menu).
- HARNESS_NAMES is imported from clauded.config so the menu mapping stays single-source-of-truth.
